title: Agentic code-review capability (off-by-default, source-separated); dogfood on rebar via Gerrit LLM-Review gate
plan:
# Agentic code-review capability for rebar (off by default, source-separated), dogfooded via the Gerrit LLM-Review gate

## Context
Build the agentic code-review capability as a SEPARATE, OFF-BY-DEFAULT rebar capability whose source is clearly isolated so it has no effect when disabled. It uses the three-pass review structure (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic gate) and is enforced through Gerrit's `LLM-Review` label on the AWS-hosted environment. Dogfooding on rebar itself is the success criterion.

## Success Criteria
- Code review runs as the rebar MCP server's `LLM-Review` voter in Gerrit, off by default and source-isolated (inert and clearly non-applicable when the feature flag is off).
- rebar's own changes are reviewed and gated by it end-to-end through the Gerrit gate (dogfood).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capability is OFF by default (config/env gated) and SOURCE-SEPARATED into its own module(s), so the code path is inert and unmistakably non-applicable when disabled.
- [ ] Uses the three-pass framework from the reviewer-migration epic (pass-1 evidence -> pass-2 verifier agent -> pass-3 deterministic typed gate).
- [ ] Provider-agnostic via rebar's existing multi-provider runner; engine = native `review_code` (an optional PR-Agent runner may sit behind the runner seam but is not required).
- [ ] Wired as the Gerrit `LLM-Review` voter: on patchset-created, review the change, run pass-1/2/3, and vote `LLM-Review` via REST; the deterministic block/advisory outcome maps to the label.
- [ ] Hard auto-block reserved for high-precision categories (secrets / High-Critical security); everything else advisory at the starting (high) thresholds.
- [ ] Dogfood proof: at least one real rebar change is reviewed + gated end-to-end on the AWS/Gerrit environment.

title: Plan-review verification gate (open->in_progress)
plan:
# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)

## Context

rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).

This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).

Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.

## Design summary

- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
  network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
  (`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
  the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
  fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity + ticket LEVEL/TYPE** (proportionate scrutiny — see below), NOT ticket type alone.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
  polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
  (per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
  and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model:** per-criterion PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL + a review-level INDETERMINATE (budget/tool-unavailable).
  Sound checks block; judgment is advisory; AMBIGUOUS escalates to a higher-tier adjudicator. A per-criterion
  config knob lets a project make a judgment criterion BLOCKING (= block-with-`--force`-override); INDETERMINATE
  is non-blocking by default (project-configurable).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
  (research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
  in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
  suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
  AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
  chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
  criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.
- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD):** Layer-1 wraps external tools, ALL added as NEW integrations by this
  epic (none are wired into rebar today): LSP resolution via solidlsp (reachable through the repo's configured
  Serena MCP server, but NOT yet a rebar library) + ast-grep (resolution/callers); syft + per-ecosystem registry +
  LLM-fallback (deps); scc + lizard (size). Unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout → skipped = PASS, never FAIL.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
  chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
  ~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
  criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
  (system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
  size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
  INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).
- **Proportionate scrutiny (by LEVEL + TYPE; experiment-grounded round 4):** criteria carry an `applies_at` field; the orchestrator filters before chunking so leaf-implementation criteria (E6 proving-commands, G1G2/E4/A1, leaf overlays T5a/b/c) do NOT run at epic/story altitude, container criteria (G3/G4 child coverage) run only when has_children, and TYPE rules apply (bugs EXEMPT→follow-on; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal/mechanical leaf tasks). Without this the gate over-fires on mid-level stories and mechanical tasks.
- **Overlay triggering (deterministic-where-low-FP, else LLM-router; validated round 4):** T1 prior-art, T5a performance, and T5d accessibility fire from low-FP deterministic signals (bright-lines / UI-keyword count); T8 LLM-antipatterns, T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state are LLM-routed (a keyword trigger is high-FP for them — e.g. T8 keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket). T8 is in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans (it recovers structural-gap signal a generic checklist misses).
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
  "perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
  tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
  ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
  justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
  existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
  only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).

## Criteria registry (authoritative full set — v4 §5; canonical)

The complete criteria set. This list is the authority; the experiment subsets are not. A completeness guard
(`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/check_registry_coverage.py`) fails loudly if any criterion here is
missing from the implemented set, so subsetting can never silently drop one again.

**Layer 1 — DET (deterministic, blocks, fail-open):** P1 readiness-shape (`## Acceptance Criteria`, all types) ·
P2 file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep) · P3 package existence (syft+registry+LLM-fallback) · P4 oversize
signals · P5 task-DAG validity + cross-task interference · P6 AC/DD quality (compound-AND, vague-lexicon,
spec-fidelity, verify-command robustness) · P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates to T4).

**Layer 2 — judgment (advisory; single-turn / 2-step / AGENT):** F1 measurability & in-session completability ·
F4 user/problem present · E1 criteria↔description coherence + terminology + duplicates · E2 ambiguity +
undercooked + decision-deferral · E3 intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate) · E5 testing-plan completeness ·
E6 verification/termination + end-state-reachability · G5 decomposition judgment · G1+G2 edit-set/scope accuracy
(AGENT) · E4 assumption/premise verification (AGENT) · A1 anti-slop/NIH + the 7 anti-patterns (AGENT) · G3 child coverage (AGENT, container) · G4 child consistency (AGENT, container) · G6 approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection (AGENT; mechanism-correctness + best-approach check, with reviewer-generated alternatives so no rejected options reach the implementer).

**Triggered overlays (deterministic where low-FP — T1/T5a/T5d; else LLM-routed — T8/T6/T5b/T5c/T9):** T1
prior-art/novel-architecture · T2 empirical probe (red→green / spike) · T3 integration feasibility · T4
compat/destructiveness-as-explicit-choice · T5a performance · T5b reliability · T5c security · T5d accessibility ·
T5e maintainability · T6 UX non-happy-path · T7 documentation · T8 LLM/prompt antipatterns · T9 shared-state lifecycle (+ concurrency/idempotency) · T10 infrastructure/IaC · T11 data-migration/backfill safety · T12 rollout/rollback/reversibility.

**Cross-cutting:** COH coherence pass (cross-section contradictions) · BROAD bounded open-ended pass
(unknown-unknowns) · benign-reading FP filter · anti-fabrication. Routing/exec/facet/checklist per criterion live in the descriptor set (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v6.json`). Round-5 roll-ins extended A1 (7 anti-patterns), T9 (concurrency/TOCTOU), T5a (cost), T5b (observability + degraded-mode), T5c (least-privilege + secrets), E5 (change-detector tests), T4 (rollback-as-remedy), P3 (license/CVE/health), G5 (consume P4 + vertical-slice MVP sequencing). Coverage vs 2025-2026 LLM-plan failure-mode research + full gap analysis: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-coverage-and-gaps.md.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Claiming a ticket (open→in_progress) requires a valid, non-stale signed plan-review attestation; absence or
      a material-edit-invalidated signature blocks the claim, with a recorded `--force` override available.
- [ ] The deterministic Layer-1 floor blocks only on sound, unambiguous checks and fails open on any unsupported
      language/tool (skipped = pass), with coverage recorded on the attestation.
- [ ] Layer-2 review returns coaching findings that are structurally well-formed — each carries criterion id,
      file/section location, severity, a checklist line, and a suggested fix when confident — verifiable in-session
      against the REVIEW_RESULT schema; judgment criteria are advisory by default. (Remediation-rate uplift is
      tracked OFFLINE as follow-on F1, not an in-session gate.)
- [ ] A project can configure risk posture, chunking, budget caps, and per-criterion blocking via config, with
      sensible defaults shipped.
- [ ] Every review emits a `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar event sufficient to reconstruct per-criterion FP/remediation
      analysis offline, without taxing rebar's hot paths.
- [ ] The signed attestation is checkable by an external CI process as a rigor/anti-vibe-coding signal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Layer-1 deterministic criteria implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open: readiness shape (standardized
      `## Acceptance Criteria` across all types), file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep), package existence
      (syft + registry + fallback), oversize signals, task-DAG validity, AC/DD quality, destructive sniff.
- [ ] Layer-2 advisory criteria implemented: measurability/agent-completable, user/problem present, criteria↔
      description coherence, ambiguity, intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate), testing-plan completeness,
      verification/termination, edit-set/scope accuracy (tool-primed), child coverage + consistency (containers),
      assumption/premise verification, anti-slop, and T2 empirical-probe (the design's existing empirical-validation overlay: a complex/novel plan with an unvalidated assumption should plan a spike/probe/measurement — SUPPRESSED when the ticket already includes experimentation; LLM-routed); plus triggered overlays (deterministic or LLM-routed) and a bounded broad pass.
- [ ] Criteria registry (declarative descriptors) + generic orchestrator, reusing rebar.llm extension points.
- [ ] Attestation: signing + material-event-commit binding + invalidation-on-material-edit + coverage record,
      reusing the close-gate signing machinery (HMAC under the MCP environment key) — integrity/forgery/replay/
      staleness model inherited, no new key custody.
- [ ] `claim` integration: fast signed-attestation check with `--force`+justification override.
- [ ] Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by
      size+complexity+centrality (centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time); per-plan budget cap with
      chunk-atomic checkpointing and an INDETERMINATE outcome (non-blocking default, configurable).
- [ ] Feedback-authoring conforms to the coaching spec (grounded/specific/ranked/affirming/checklist).
- [ ] `REVIEW_RESULT` reducer-ignored sidecar event with finding fingerprints; reducer skips the type; override
      logging; offline-analysis-ready.
- [ ] Per-criterion config: advisory (default) | blocking-with-override.
- [ ] Bugs are exempt from this gate (routed to a follow-on).
- [x] Defaults for chunking + budget + depth grounded in the experiment results (see `## Experiment-grounded defaults` below + docs/experiments/).
- [ ] Tests + verification: every AC maps to a concrete proving check; fail-open is tested per tool (unsupported-
      lang / no-server / crash / timeout → skipped=pass), plus tests for signature invalidation-on-material-edit,
      `--force` override logging, cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first), and the reducer-ignored
      REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (reducer skips the type; head/.cache/deps/validate unaffected).
- [ ] Docs: how the gate works, the config surface, fail-open behavior, the cert semantics, and the CI-check pattern.

## Experiment-grounded defaults (round 3; config-overridable)

Grounded by 5 experiment rounds (~1,600 LLM runs). Full design: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-storage-and-chunking.md.

- **Execution tiers are the primary axis (not chunk size).** DET (P1–P7) = parallel code, ~free. SINGLE-TURN
  (F1,F4,E2,E5,G5,T5a–e) = chunkable LLM calls, ~$0.005–0.008/criterion cached. 2-STEP (E1,E3,E6) = two ordered
  calls. AGENT/tool-using (E4,G1G2,A1,T1,T6–T8) = one agent loop per criterion, ~$0.12 + ~50s each (≈85× a
  single-turn call) — NOT chunkable; gated by proportionate scrutiny.
- **Criteria storage:** each descriptor declares `exec` (tier), `facet` (the concern/context it examines — the
  advisory grouping key), `context_needs`, `routing`, `trigger`. The chunker is generic: filter → bucket by tier
  → pack same-`facet` single-turn criteria up to the chunk size → one agent per AGENT criterion → DET as code.
  Groupings are advisory (facet tags re-pack at any chunk size); adding/tuning a criterion only declares exec+facet.
- **Chunk size = base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket), within the single-turn tier.** base_chunk = {opus 12,
  sonnet 6 with coherent facet packing (3 without), haiku/local 3}; size_factor = {trivial/moderate 1.0,
  large|epic|has_children 0.5}; clamp [2, n]. → Opus packs ~all single-turn criteria in one cached call (its
  consistency is flat in N); Sonnet 6 (coherent) / 3 (large); never the full set on the largest plans.
- **Enable prompt caching** (system+plan as the cached prefix): collapses the cost gap between chunk sizes (1.55×
  vs 2.6×), so chunk for reliability not cost. Cached fine-chunk Sonnet (~$0.07–0.09/review) ≈ one Opus pass
  (~$0.10) — prefer Opus for max consistency/big-plan stability, Sonnet+cache+coherent for the cost floor.
- **Budget (generous, per-plan):** cap = $0.15 (DET+single-turn base) + n_agentic_fired × $0.25 + headroom (small
  task ~$0.15–0.40; heavy epic ~$1.5–2.0 + minutes). INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds lowest-priority AGENT/overlay
  criteria first (85× cost), never the cheap base.
- **Criteria are fully specified from DSO source** (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md), not one-liners,
  and the set is >12 (single-turn ~13 + agentic + overlays + DET P1–P7).

## Registry reconciliation (post-round-4)

The round-4 'EXP' criterion was the design's existing **T2 empirical probe** reinvented under a wrong name. Reconciling v4 §5 against what was implemented found other dropped criteria: **T3** integration feasibility and **T4** compat/destructiveness (never implemented), **T5d** accessibility (dropped from the criteria set), the dedicated **coherence pass**, and **G3/G4** container child-coverage (designed, never run). All are restored to the corrected criteria set (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/criteria_v5.json) and the full v4 §5 registry is the canonical source. See docs/experiments/criteria-registry-reconciliation.md.

## Design decisions revisited (round 5; SETTLED in the brainstorm)

A round-5 external review re-raised two design questions — but both were already the brainstorm's central debates (session log 63cc, DISCUSSION NARRATIVE + final-decisions entry), resolved with explicit reasoning; they are NOT open.
(1) **Blocking on `claim` vs coaching** — the brainstorm explicitly weighed A blocking-inline / B advisory / C decoupled-signed-attestation and chose **C**: `claim` is a FAST succeed/fail check for a signed 'ready' attestation, the heavy review runs out-of-band, `--force`+justification is the escape. The 'hard-gate-is-anti-pattern' research was examined and found NOT to transfer to autonomous agents (which confabulate rather than ask, and where early errors compound; separate-critic >> self-critique). The minimal sound DET floor is the only hard blocker; Layer-2 never blocks. (2) **What the signature certifies** — an Opus adversarial review in the brainstorm raised the same 'fail-open hides a hollow signal' critique; the resolution is that the cert means 'process followed, no red flags' (NOT 'perfect') WITH **coverage recorded**, the rich per-criterion verdicts/fingerprints live in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, and a project composes any hard CI gate by checking the signed result + coverage. The brainstorm also explicitly rejected block-by-default-with-routine-override (>30% FP → tool ignored) in favor of advisory-Layer-2.
The genuinely-useful RESIDUAL from the round-5 review (tuning, not redesign): state end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check); the prompt-cache busts on the edit-then-re-review loop (re-derive that saving); and make the CI rigor predicate canonical in-repo rather than leaving each client to define it. Notably the review's findings map to COH (a criterion we'd dropped) and G6 (the one we added) — evidence the registry is on track.

## Self-review note (gate run on this epic, round 3)

The full gate was run on this epic per the default protocol (DET floor pass; Opus single-turn facet-chunks;
Sonnet agentic tier with real rebar-repo tools). It surfaced — and this revision addresses — the false 'already
in repo' tooling claim (E4/G1G2, caught only by the agentic tier), a missing test/verification AC (E5/E6), an
unnamed invocation surface (broad pass), an unmeasurable success criterion (F1), and a missing claim-path latency
target (T5a). The gate's **G5 finding is CORRECT and intentionally left open**: this epic should be decomposed —
the explicit NEXT action is to split it into the one-wave implementation plan (child tasks with deps). FP rate
was low (F4/E1/E2/E3/A1 passed); see docs/experiments/ for the baseline assessment.

## Follow-on work (to be filed as separate linked tickets, NOT part of this epic)

F1 FP/remediation-rate tracking tooling (offline analysis on REVIEW_RESULT) · F2 auto-demote blocking criteria on
FP ceiling · F3 confidence/severity floor for blocking · F4 promote checks to blocking-by-default as low-FP ones
are observed · F5 project configuration tool/UX · F6 bug-ticket review (dedicated epic) · F7 cross-ticket
no-overlap / dependency-aware semantic detection · F8 project-supplied criteria + project-invariant compliance ·
F9 code-drift invalidation · F10 calibrated abstention + labeled gold set · F11 rigor-signature chain / CI
integration helpers · F12 severity-minor-floor + NOT-flag tuning.

title: Tier E: delete the bash dispatcher -> argparse cli.py + port ~20 live cmds + zero _engine/*.sh
plan:
## Why
Tier E is the LAST tier of the bash->Python strangler-fig migration (story adult-oxide-slave,
8784-c2d7-c395-4478). Tiers A-D are DONE/retired; Python is the sole live impl for reads, leaf
writes, compute, and the write/sync core. The story's end-state AC is literally "no _engine/*.sh":
one implementation + three facades (lib/CLI/MCP), zero standalone shell tests, no embedded heredocs.

Grounding against the live tree (2026-06-12) shows this requires porting ~20 STILL-LIVE bash commands
the dispatcher routes to (init, fsck, fsck-recover, transition, reopen, claim, compact, compact-all,
scratch, clarity-check, check-ac, quality-check, summary, exists, resolve, format, list-descendants,
delete, get-file-impact, get-verify-commands, bridge-status, purge-bridge) -- not just deleting dead
wrappers -- plus rewiring the reconciler off the compat shims + engine_env, relocating ticket_txn.py
off the bare _engine/event_append.py, deleting the dispatcher + compat shims, and translating all 107
.sh test files to pytest. Plan reviewed by opus subagent (REVISE->APPROVE).

## What
Execute the approved cluster plan, ONE COMMAND AT A TIME (the established §2 per-command protocol:
characterize -> port reusing rebar.reducer/graph/_commands/_store/_engine_support -> byte-parity
goldens -> cut library+argparse to in-process -> dogfood soak -> delete bash + translate suite), each
in a clean green commit. Tier E has NO kill-switch; rollback is git revert. Clusters:
- E0: argparse cli.py skeleton + help/usage/error goldens (byte-identical to the bash dispatcher via a
  golden-as-package-data help action) + the auto-init/freshness middleware (port _ensure_initialized).
  Transitional subprocess fallback for not-yet-ported commands. NO deletions.
- E1: cut the console_scripts entrypoint over to argparse (dispatcher still exists for fallbacks).
- E2: port read-ish/quality cmds (clarity-check, check-ac, quality-check, summary, exists, resolve,
  format, list-descendants, get-file-impact, get-verify-commands, delete).
- E3: port write/lifecycle cmds (transition, reopen, claim, compact, compact-all, scratch).
- E4: port init / fsck / fsck-recover (highest risk; isolated soak each).
- E5: port bridge-status / purge-bridge.
- E5b: rewire rebar_reconciler off ticket_reducer/bare event_append/engine_env.
- E5c: relocate ticket_txn.py; delete the bare _engine/event_append.py.
- E6: rewire remaining compat-shim importers; delete the shims; assert-gone tests.
- E7: delete the dispatcher _engine/rebar + engine_env/PYTHONPATH machinery + engine_dir zipimport
  assertion + jq prereq + flock(1)/mkdir discovery; retire machinery tests in the same commit.
- E8: translate the 107 .sh suites to pytest; delete suite-engine.sh/assert.sh/collector.
- E9: docs (architecture.md offender table -> empty; bash-migration.md §7 DONE) + exhaustive
  live-dogfood validation + soak-evidence comment + close adult-oxide-slave.

## Scope
The whole remaining bash engine, one command/cluster at a time over multiple sessions; each cluster
independently green + revertible. NOT in scope: store/locking/event-schema changes (ratified sound);
dropping the dual-leg mkdir lock (release-level follow-up, see Tier D note).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cli.py is a real argparse CLI over rebar._commands/_reads/_engine_support (three facades, one impl)
- [ ] Help/usage/error output byte-identical to the bash dispatcher (goldens pinned before cutover)
- [ ] All ~20 live commands ported in-process; library no longer subprocesses the dispatcher
- [ ] reconciler + ticket_txn.py rewired off compat shims / bare event_append / engine_env
- [ ] Zero _engine/*.sh; compat shims deleted with assert-gone tests; dispatcher + machinery deleted
- [ ] Zero standalone shell tests (tests/**/*.sh == 0); suites translated to pytest, suite green
- [ ] Exhaustive live-dogfood validation passed; soak-evidence recorded; adult-oxide-slave closed

title: Overlay triggering: deterministic-where-low-FP, LLM-router otherwise
plan:
# Overlay triggering: deterministic-where-low-FP, LLM-router otherwise

## Why
Running every overlay on every plan is wasteful and noisy; the router decides relevance. Some overlays have a reliable deterministic trigger (keyword/signal) and should fire that way to avoid an LLM call; the rest need a judgment pass.

## What
A two-path overlay trigger: a deterministic detector where a low-FP signal exists, with an LLM relevance pass as the fallback/for ambiguous cases.

## Scope
- Overlays (the full v4 §5 set — do not subset): T1 prior-art/novel-arch, T2 empirical-probe, T3 integration-feasibility, T4 compat/destructiveness, T5a perf, T5b reliability, T5c security, T5d accessibility, T5e maintainability, T6 UX, T7 docs, T8 LLM-antipatterns, T9 shared-state. (T2/T3/T4/T5d were dropped from an earlier experiment subset and restored — see criteria-registry-reconciliation.md; T3/T4/T5d still lack coverage data.), T10 infrastructure/IaC, T11 data-migration/backfill safety, T12 rollout/rollback/reversibility (T10 deterministic-triggered on IaC keywords; T11/T12 on data-change / deployed-system signals).
- Deterministic triggers (low-FP, VALIDATED round 4 — agree 12-13/13 with the LLM router, ~0 false-positive): T1 prior-art <- the 6 bright-lines; T5a performance <- I/O/compute/LLM-call/batch signals; T5d accessibility <- UI-keyword count.
- LLM-router relevance pass for the overlays whose keyword trigger is HIGH-FP (validated round 4): T8 LLM-antipatterns (keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket — 9/13 fire but only 3 relevant), T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state; plus confirming ambiguous deterministic hits. T8 MUST be in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans — it recovers structural-gap signal (enum-vocabulary/protocol-co-location gaps) a generic checklist misses.
- Calibration: track each overlay's downstream dismissal rate (over/under-triggering) per the observability substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A deterministic trigger per overlay that has a low-FP signal, unit-tested for precision on fixtures.
- [ ] An LLM relevance pass covering the remaining overlays + ambiguous deterministic hits.
- [ ] Each overlay descriptor declares whether it triggers deterministically or by LLM, in the registry.
- [ ] Overlay fire/dismissal is recorded in REVIEW_RESULT for offline calibration.

title: Contract freeze (in-window): exit-code contract, error envelope everywhere, event-schema versioning
plan:
## Why
Rec 6 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md. The JSON Schema system (SSOT schemas, $ref-shared common.schema.json, coverage guard test_schema_coverage.py) is a strength — keep it. But three contracts agents depend on are unfrozen, and the breaking-change window after the 2026-06-09 internal announcement is the cheap time to freeze them: a 500-person rollout soon means mixed rebar versions sharing one origin/tickets.

## What
(a) Exit-code contract: 0/1/2/10 are load-bearing for agents but scattered through script headers — author one canonical doc (docs/exit-codes.md or a section in output-schemas.md) + a conformance test.
(b) Error envelope: extend the error_envelope schema to every command's failure mode so agents never parse stderr prose; error-shape changes are breaking — decide now.
(c) Event-schema versioning: docs/event-schema.md is the true wire format BETWEEN CLONES of different rebar versions. Declare it formally versioned; add + test a reducer rule that unknown event TYPEs are preserved-and-ignored (forward compat).
(d) Close the mcp_server.py pydantic-model-vs-JSON-schema equivalence pin fully (test_schema_outputs.py is partial).

## Scope
Contracts and docs + their conformance tests; no behavioral redesign. Anything that changes an emitted error shape lands inside the window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical exit-code contract documented + conformance-tested
- [ ] error_envelope covers every command failure mode; documented as the only machine-readable error channel
- [ ] Event schema carries an explicit version + tested unknown-TYPE forward-compat rule
- [ ] MCP outputSchemas pinned equivalent to the JSON Schemas in CI

title: E7: delete the bash dispatcher + engine (E7e reconciler/validate off REBAR_TICKET_CLI first, then delete .sh+helpers+shims), then E9 docs+close
plan:
## Why
Final step of Tier E: delete the bash engine so rebar has ONE implementation (Python) + three facades. All in-process ports (E0-E6.5a), shim rewires (E6a/E7a), GAP translations (E8), and the bash-dependent test side (E7c) are DONE. The in-process CLI no longer touches the dispatcher (rebar._run/_passthrough are dead). What remains: sever the last two RUNTIME consumers of the dispatcher, then delete the engine, then docs+close.

## E7e (BLOCKER — must land before deleting the dispatcher)
The reconciler (which STAYS) and validate still subprocess the bash dispatcher to read tickets:
- engine_env() sets REBAR_TICKET_CLI = dispatcher(); read in rebar_reconciler/{applier.py:406, invariants.py:26, reconcile.py:447} -> runs [cli_path, list/show/...].
- _engine_support/validate.py:_default_ticket_cmd() returns engine_dir()/ticket; run_checks -> check_interface_contracts subprocesses it (even when TICKET_CMD unset = production).
Fix: add src/rebar/__main__.py (python -m rebar -> rebar._cli.main), repoint REBAR_TICKET_CLI + _default_ticket_cmd at the in-process CLI (console script via shutil.which, or [sys.executable,'-m','rebar']; callers treat it as one path so __main__.py + console-script path is cleanest). Then RE-RUN a live reconciler dry-run vs DIG to confirm it still reads local tickets (LIVE-DIG authorized).

## E7-final (delete, one commit, after E7e green)
DELETE: _engine/rebar + ticket; all 36 _engine/*.sh; dispatcher-only helpers (ticket-reducer.py, ticket-graph.py, ticket-reads.py, ticket-commands.py, ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py, ticket-alias-compute.py, ticket-alias-resolve.py, ticket-list-descendants.py, ticket-unblock.py, ticket-bridge-fsck.py); the 5 shims (ticket_reducer/, ticket_graph/, ticket_reads.py, ticket_resolver.py, ticket_output.py); ticket_txn.py.
KEEP: _engine/rebar_reconciler/, jira-capability-probe.py, resources/ticket-wordlist.txt.
_engine.py: delete dispatcher() + run(); KEEP engine_dir()/engine_env()/wordlist_path() (reconciler+probe+_alias need them); drop the REBAR_TICKET_CLI=dispatcher() line in engine_env.
__init__.py: delete dead _run() + the run/dispatcher import. _cli/__init__.py: delete dead _passthrough() + its dispatcher import.
tests/unit/test_engine_dir.py: change dispatcher().is_file() assertion -> engine_dir/wordlist/reconciler exist (KEEP the rest; engine_dir survives + the no-top-level-names guard matters more now). _engine_path.py SURVIVES.
Modernize stderr usage strings that still say 'Usage: ticket-list-descendants.py' / 'ticket-reads.py' (descendants.py:80, reads.py:760) -> rebar form (byte-parity tests already deleted, so safe).

## E9
docs/architecture.md offender table -> empty + engine-import-boundary note; docs/bash-migration.md §7 -> DONE; note the help golden is now _cli/_help-anchored. Exhaustive dogfood (3 interfaces, fsck clean, reconcile dry-run). Close adult-oxide-slave then nervy-hold-dip.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E7e: src/rebar/__main__.py added; REBAR_TICKET_CLI + _default_ticket_cmd point at the in-process CLI; live reconciler dry-run vs DIG green
- [ ] Zero src/rebar/_engine/*.sh; dispatcher + 9 helpers + 5 shims + ticket_txn deleted
- [ ] engine_dir/engine_env/wordlist_path + rebar_reconciler/ + jira-capability-probe.py + wordlist KEPT and working
- [ ] _run/_passthrough/dispatcher()/run() deleted; no dead imports
- [ ] Full pytest suite green (zero tests/**/*.sh already achieved); opus-review the cutover
- [ ] E9 docs updated; adult-oxide-slave + nervy-hold-dip closed

title: Align quality-gate rubrics, remove dso dispatch language, document the ticket template
plan:
## Summary
The three per-ticket gates run fine post-extraction but their rubrics are mutually inconsistent, their expected format is documented nowhere, and one still speaks dso dispatch language. Found while validating the gates against the 2026-06-09 audit tickets (43 tickets; substantive-but-unformatted bug tickets scored 1-3/fail while format-conformant prose passed).

## Findings (verified in source)
1. Rubric inconsistency: ticket-clarity-check.sh awards the Acceptance Criteria bonus ONLY for ticket_type=task (:185-188); for epics it rewards "## Success Criteria" (:215-218) — but check-acceptance-criteria.sh requires a literal "## Acceptance Criteria" heading with "- [" items on EVERY type (:60-66). So a clarity-perfect epic still fails check-ac unless it carries BOTH headings.
2. dso residue: issue-quality-check.sh fail messages say "using inline prompt" (:17,:123,:140,:50) — that described the dso sub-agent dispatcher's fallback, which does not exist in rebar.
3. Undocumented contract: the heading rubric (Reproduction Steps for bugs, Why/What for stories, Success Criteria+Context for epics, Acceptance Criteria checklists for all) appears only in gate source; README/CLAUDE.md never tell ticket authors (i.e. agents) the expected template, so gate failures are unactionable without reading gate code.
4. Honest-labeling: clarity-check is a structure/keyword heuristic (its header says "SC2 heuristic") — it measures format conformance, not semantic clarity; trivially satisfiable by headings + 500 filler chars. Acceptable as a floor check, but docs should say so.

## Fix direction
- Make the per-type rubrics consistent (decide: AC required for all types in clarity-check too, or check-ac learns per-type headings; recommend the former — one vocabulary).
- Replace "using inline prompt" with a rebar-appropriate reason string (update any asserting tests).
- Add a "ticket template" section to CLAUDE.md/README documenting the per-type headings the gates check, so agents author conformant tickets first time.
- Document the gates as structural floor checks (not semantic scoring) where they are described.

## Suggested RED test
Rubric-consistency test: a fixture ticket per type that passes clarity-check at threshold must also pass check-ac (RED under today's epic/story rubric divergence).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One consistent heading vocabulary across clarity-check and check-ac (per-type matrix documented)
- [ ] No dso dispatch language in gate output (grep 'inline prompt' src/ clean)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md/README document the ticket template the gates enforce
- [ ] Existing gate tests updated; full suite green

title: T3 — NDJSON import core: rebar import (two-pass, composed events)
plan:
# T3 — NDJSON import core: rebar import / rebar.import_tickets() (two-pass, composed events)

## Context

P1.2 epic rule-swamp-pupil (ab74-b205-72af-4ce1). Consume our own NDJSON and reproduce the
same logical state in a fresh repo via the NORMAL locked write path (no raw-event
injection). Depends on T1 (provenance plumbing) and T2 (NDJSON format + schema to consume).
Idempotency + deferred-push perf are split into T4; this ticket delivers a correct working
importer (re-runs may duplicate — that gap is closed by T4 and documented here).

## What

- New `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`, `src/rebar/_io/_provenance.py`.
- `rebar.import_tickets(...)` library fn + `rebar import` CLI (dispatch in
  `_cli/__init__.py`, help `_cli/help/import.txt`, `--dry-run`, `--output`).
- Two-pass:
  - Pass 1: `create_ticket(type, title, description, priority, tags, assignee)` for every
    line (do NOT set parent in CREATE — parent must be open/in_progress); capture
    `{source_id → new local_id}` id-map. Pass source_* (T1) into the CREATE.
  - Pass 2 (per ticket): `edit_ticket(parent=…)`; `link(…)` (dedup by
    `(source,target,relation)`); `transition(open→in_progress|closed)` to reproduce non-open
    STATUS; `comment(…)` with per-comment source fields; `set_file_impact`/`set_verify_commands`.
- Dangling parent/link target not in the import set → skip + warn (never fail the run).
- `_provenance.py` maps export line → create/comment kwargs (source_* extraction).

## Scope

In: correct two-pass importer + STATUS reproduction + dry-run + dangling skip. Out:
idempotent skip-by-source_id, deferred-push perf, large-import docs (all T4).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Round-trip: `export` a store then `import` into a fresh repo reproduces the same
      logical state (status, assignee, parent, links, tags, comments, file_impact,
      verify_commands) modulo fresh ids, with original date/author preserved as `source_*`.
- [ ] Non-open statuses (closed/in_progress) reproduced via STATUS events.
- [ ] Provenance surfaced on imported tickets/comments (source_id/created_at/author/env).
- [ ] Dangling parent/link target → skipped with a warning; run still succeeds.
- [ ] `--dry-run` reports create counts without writing.
- [ ] Tests: round-trip ≡ logical state, status reproduction, provenance surfacing,
      dangling-link skip, dry-run; `pytest` green.

title: WS-1: Pluggable output contract + completion_verdict schema (+ BI-1)
plan:
Framework seam: make the agent's structured-output **contract pluggable per operation**, and add the `completion_verdict` contract + schema. The runner currently hardcodes `findings_response_model()`; this is the enabling change (epic req #4).

## Scope (files)
- `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` (NEW): name→response-model registry; `response_model_for(output_schema)` (default findings); import-clean (pydantic only inside builders); `completion_verdict_response_model` defined here.
- `src/rebar/llm/findings.py`: extract `citation_model()`/`finding_model()` lazy builders (shared Citation); promote `_validate_structured`→public `validate_structured`; **BI-1**: `finalize_outcome(mode="structured")` uses `model_dump(exclude_none=True)`.
- `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`: both runners select response model via `contracts.response_model_for(req.output_schema)` (lazy import inside run()); add `RunRequest.extra_tools` appended to the tool list (name-collision check).
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` (NEW, full-URL `$id`) + register `COMPLETION_VERDICT`/`OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["verify_completion"]`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `response_model_for(None)`→findings; `("completion_verdict")`→CompletionVerdict; unknown→findings default
- [ ] schema-pin test: CompletionVerdict mirrors `completion_verdict` schema; existing findings pin still passes
- [ ] BI-1: a model with omitted optionals serializes without nulls and validates (real-model test, not FakeRunner)
- [ ] `import rebar.llm` pulls no pydantic (import-cleanliness); existing review/code/scan ops unchanged
- [ ] `extra_tools` appended in both runners with a duplicate-name guard

title: clarity_check returns schema-violating {output,...} shape on missing ticket; siblings degrade properly
plan:
## Summary
clarity_check returns a schema-violating {"output",...} dict on a missing ticket; sibling gates degrade with proper shapes; over MCP it surfaces as a pydantic ValidationError instead of a clean error.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar.clarity_check("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"output": "ERROR: failed to retrieve ticket ...", "passed": false}
2. rebar.check_ac("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"verdict":"fail","criteria_count":0,"reason":"could not load ...","passed":false}
3. Over MCP: call_tool("clarity_check", ...) -> ToolError: 3 validation errors for ClarityResultOut (score/verdict/threshold missing).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: one consistent missing-ticket contract across all three gates and across library/MCP (either RebarError like show_ticket, or a schema-conforming fail result).
- Actual: clarity_check alone violates clarity_result.schema.json and explodes the MCP output model.

## Source
- src/rebar/__init__.py:178-183 — fallback dict shape {"output", "passed"}.
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:211-214 — ClarityResultOut rejects it.

## Fix direction
On engine exit 2 raise RebarError (consistent with show_ticket), or return {"score":0,"verdict":"fail","threshold":N} — pick ONE contract and apply to all three gates. See validation notes comment for the recommended option.

## Suggested RED test
All three gates on a nonexistent id, via library AND MCP; assert the single chosen contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] clarity_check missing-ticket result validates against clarity_result.schema.json OR raises RebarError — consistent with check_ac/quality_check after the change
- [ ] MCP clarity_check on missing id returns a clean ToolError or valid payload (no pydantic stack)
- [ ] RED test first; parity asserted across library + MCP

title: Status/compact/claim/delete writes commit to tickets branch but never push (PUSH_PENDING when last op)
plan:
## Summary

The inline-commit write paths — `transition`/`reopen`/`claim` (txn.py), `compact` (compact.py), and `delete` (delete.py) — commit their events to the local `tickets` branch but **never call the auto-push**. Only the `append_event` family (`create`/`comment`/`edit`/`tag`/`untag`/`set_*` via `write_and_push`) and `link` (graph/_links.py) push. Because a push sends the whole `HEAD:tickets`, transition/compact/claim commits are normally *carried* to origin by the next push-capable write — so the gap is invisible until such a write is the **last** operation of a session (e.g. closing an epic), which strands its commits as `PUSH_PENDING`.

This contradicts the documented contract in CLAUDE.md: 'Every write (create/edit/transition/claim/link/…) auto-commits its event to the tickets branch AND auto-pushes to origin/tickets.'

## Reproduction Steps

In a fresh rebar repo with an `origin` remote:
1. `create` a ticket  → origin advances (push fires). ahead=0
2. `claim` it          → origin unchanged. ahead=1
3. `transition` closed → origin unchanged. ahead=3 (claim+status+compact)
4. `comment`           → origin advances, carrying all 4. ahead=0

Empirically reproduced deterministically in a clean repo (no concurrency, healthy origin), so it is a push **absence**, not a push **failure**.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: every write (incl. transition/claim/reopen/compact/delete) auto-pushes `HEAD:tickets` best-effort, per the CLAUDE.md contract.
- Actual: transition/claim/reopen/compact/delete commit but do not push; they rely on a later append_event write to carry them. A trailing status/compact strands commits (fsck → PUSH_PENDING).

## Root cause

The bash `write_commit_event` auto-pushed on EVERY write. The tier-E bash→Python migration (commits e6e645ae 'relocate transition/claim write core into rebar._commands.txn', 41217cc7 'port transition/reopen', 9f7bd246 'port compact/compact-all', ~2026-06-13) gave these paths their own inline rename+commit but omitted the push that write_and_push performs. NOT caused by 0b32 (1dbe5e7e, canonical bytes) or 7d1d (cee1fe98, HLC) — those threaded canonical-bytes/HLC through txn/compact on 2026-06-17 but preserved the (already-missing) push wiring; pickaxe -S'push' on txn.py/compact.py/transition.py shows no push call was ever present or removed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `transition`/`reopen`/`claim`/`compact`/`delete` trigger a best-effort push of `HEAD:tickets` after their locked commit (after lock release), honoring the `sync.push` policy (always/async/off).
- [ ] A transition/compact that is the LAST op of a session leaves origin/tickets current (no PUSH_PENDING in fsck).
- [ ] Push remains best-effort: a push failure never fails the write.
- [ ] No double-push regression for the append_event/link paths that already push.
- [ ] Test: in a repo with an origin, a lone `transition`/`claim`/`compact` (no following append_event write) leaves ahead=0.

title: Access control: Google SSO (Option B) for audit-snap.solutions.navateam.com
plan:
Put audit-snap.solutions.navateam.com behind Google OAuth so it is reachable ONLY by an authenticated @navapbc.com user. Implemented via OPTION B — a central auth host + a domain-wide signed session cookie (true SSO, reusable by future *.solutions.navateam.com dashboards) — chosen 2026-06-09.

CURRENT STATE: the dashboard is a static SPA on S3 + CloudFront (private origin via OAC) + Route53 + the shared *.solutions.navateam.com wildcard ACM cert. Distribution id E3R2VUIP5HOP80 (account 896586841071, us-east-1). NO auth today — fully public. Infra is Terraform under infra/shared (audit_dashboard.tf); published by .github/workflows/publish-audit-dashboard.yml.

ARCHITECTURE (Option B):
- A dedicated auth host auth.solutions.navateam.com performs the Google OAuth 2.0 code flow (the ONLY host registered with Google; single redirect URI .../_callback). It verifies the ID-token hd/email = navapbc.com, then sets an HMAC-signed session cookie scoped to Domain=.solutions.navateam.com.
- Each protected subdomain runs a viewer-request Lambda@Edge (us-east-1) that validates the shared signed cookie; if absent/expired it 302s to the auth host /authorize with a validated return_to (open-redirect-safe: return_to must be a *.solutions.navateam.com host).
- navapbc.com = Google Workspace; consent screen User type = Internal (org restriction) + server-side hd/email check (defense-in-depth).
- Google forbids wildcard redirect URIs; the central-auth pattern is what makes one client cover all subdomains.

SECRETS: Google client_secret + a cookie-signing secret stored in AWS Secrets Manager / SSM SecureString. Lambda@Edge cannot use env vars or low-latency secret calls at viewer-request, so config is baked into the function bundle at deploy time.

DEPENDENCY: a Google OAuth client (Internal consent, redirect https://auth.solutions.navateam.com/_callback) must be provisioned by Nava IT — brief delivered 2026-06-09; client_id + client_secret pending.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An unauthenticated request to audit-snap.solutions.navateam.com is redirected to Google sign-in (no dashboard content served).
- [ ] A non-@navapbc.com Google account is denied (server-side hd/email check), with a clear message; only @navapbc.com users reach the dashboard.
- [ ] After signing in once, the session is carried across subdomains via the .solutions.navateam.com signed cookie (SSO) and a second protected host requires no re-login.
- [ ] return_to is validated to a *.solutions.navateam.com host (no open redirect); session cookie is HMAC-signed, HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, with a bounded lifetime.
- [ ] The whole thing is Terraform-managed under infra/shared, reusable so another subdomain opts in with one block; applied to prod; a runbook documents adoption + rotation.
- [ ] No regression to dashboard publishing (publish-audit-dashboard.yml still deploys content).

title: Class A: portable user-navigation coverage engine (explore-then-replay)
plan:
## Context
The audit OBSERVE engine reaches surfaces via a HARD-CODED route manifest + seed-to-URL shortcuts. This is non-portable (encodes this prototype's routes) and non-hermetic-in-effect: when a screen is added/renamed it is silently MISSED, so verdicts flip on capture-presence (RC-D) rather than app behavior. Run-0036 experiments proved it: a zero-literal crawl (run-artifacts/run-0036/harness/exp_crawler.mjs) reached /apply/cancel, /apply, and the file-now decision page is served-but-UNBOUND (the SNAP-MGR1 hotspot), AND a hand-rolled forward-driver stalls at form validation. The OSS survey converged on EXPLORE-THEN-REPLAY: an LLM-driven semantic pass (ARIA-tree/ref handles) builds a frozen coverage map once; a ZERO-LLM deterministic replay drives scored runs; self-heal only on real drift (Stagehand actWithCache, browser-use workflow-use, Skyvern script-gen). This epic builds that, replacing the route-list + seed shortcuts with user-like navigation that reaches whatever the app serves.

## Why (hotspots this fixes)
RC-D capture-presence flippers: N4 (ESAP seed never lands), SNAP-MGR1 (file-now outside the manifest), MGR3-IR5 + MGR-CS (operative/terminal surfaces only intermittently captured). Also unblocks G7 (per-element capture) + MGR-CS (deterministic MD cert capture).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A scored gather reaches EVERY user-reachable screen the app serves (including form/branch-gated screens) with ZERO route/field/seed literals in the engine.
- [ ] Coverage is DETERMINISTIC across independent runs (identical surface set) -> no capture-presence verdict flips.
- [ ] An added/renamed screen is reached automatically with no manifest edit (regression of the exp_crawler finding).
- [ ] HERMETIC: the LLM is confined to an offline map-building step; scored runs are zero-LLM replay; the frozen map carries resolved semantic handles + descriptions, no app-specific literals or stored verdicts.
- [ ] PORTABLE: demonstrated on a second, structurally-different prototype with zero engine changes.
- [ ] The RC-D hotspots (N4/SNAP-MGR1/MGR3-IR5/MGR-CS) stabilize under deterministic capture.

## References
ADR-0033 Consequences (deferred Class A); run-artifacts/run-0036/harness/exp_crawler.mjs + exp_crawler2.mjs; the OSS research report (browser-use, Stagehand, Skyvern, Playwright-MCP, crawl4ai/Firecrawl, WebArena/WebVoyager) in SESSION-LOG-2026-06-13.md.

title: Stand up central auth host auth.solutions.navateam.com (OAuth code flow)
plan:
Terraform (infra/shared, us-east-1) for the central auth host auth.solutions.navateam.com: CloudFront + Lambda@Edge (or a Lambda Function URL fronted by CloudFront) + Route53 A/AAAA, reusing the *.solutions.navateam.com wildcard ACM cert. Endpoints: /authorize (build the Google authorize URL with a signed state carrying return_to), /_callback (exchange code, verify ID-token hd + email end in navapbc.com, set the HMAC-signed session cookie Domain=.solutions.navateam.com HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Lax, redirect to validated return_to), /_logout (clear the cookie). Reject non-navapbc with a clear 403 page. Config (client_id, secret, signing key) baked from Secrets Manager at deploy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] auth.solutions.navateam.com serves /authorize, /_callback, /_logout; full Google code flow works for a navapbc.com user.
- [ ] hd/email != navapbc.com is rejected; return_to is restricted to *.solutions.navateam.com (no open redirect).
- [ ] Signed cookie set on .solutions.navateam.com (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, bounded lifetime).

title: Post-commit optimization: cross-state isolated-environment parallelization (~3x, safe) + --timing diagnostic + per-request infra/seed levers — audit-run wall-clock reduction
plan:
CONTEXT
Captured post-run-0041 (commit 21fa061c) while reasoning about how to shrink audit-run wall clock. A full conformance sweep is ~4h per state; the question was whether parallelism can cut that. The analysis below was produced during that session (which was cut off before this ticket was filed) and is folded here as a single deferred, post-commit optimization ticket. Filed P3 (deferred optimization): no behavior is broken, and nothing here changes for an in-flight run — switching execution model mid-run would break hermeticity.

SCOPE — three optimization levers bundled here
1. Cross-state isolated-environment parallelization (the safe, highest-leverage item).
2. A `--timing` diagnostic (per-phase / per-step wall-clock instrumentation) to find where a single state's ~4h actually goes.
3. Per-request infra speedups + the O(N^2)->O(N) seed question (a fidelity trade, see below).

LEVER 1 — cross-state isolated-environment parallelization (SAFE; ~3x; do this)
Run each state's conformance on its OWN isolated environment (separate host/CPU + its own dev server + its own DB). Because nothing is shared across states, their timing is not coupled, so the k>=3 determinism gate and the measurement-stability controls are unaffected. This caps the safe win at the x-states factor (~3x for the current 3-state set). Reliability-neutral-to-positive and rule-compliant.

THE TRAP — intra-host / shared-resource parallelism (do NOT, without proof; likely counterproductive)
Parallelizing states on ONE host, or langs / surfaces / replicates within a state, all share CPU + the single dev server + the single DB, which couples their timing. That is dangerous specifically here:
- It threatens the k>=3 determinism gate and the measurement-stability controls. Concurrent load makes replicates sample a LOADED condition, which can either mask real non-determinism or manufacture spurious divergence. The determinism guarantee rests on INDEPENDENT samples; concurrent replicates are not independent.
- It is concrete, not abstract: the deep-replay dropped-step (Ticket c460-93f4-66cb-4aa9) is load-sensitive. Adding concurrent load would raise the flake rate -> more 2/3 arrival STOPs -> more full ~4h re-runs. Net effect is plausibly SLOWER and less reliable — the opposite of the goal.
- The dev Rails server is itself a bottleneck (limited dev threads), so intra-host parallelism would partly serialize at the server anyway while still paying the contention cost.

THE ADOPTION GATE (how the risky kind could earn its way in)
Parallelism is not categorically forbidden — the rule is "must prove it changes nothing first." The harness already ships the equivalence instrument: a parallel configuration is adoptable for a SCORED run only if a state's conformance under it reproduces the serial baseline's content-signature.json AND passes the k>=3 determinism + measurement positive controls BYTE-IDENTICALLY. If the parallel config perturbs any of those, the drift sentinel / determinism gate catches it — and that IS the rejection signal. Cross-state-on-isolated-resources passes this trivially (nothing shared); intra-host parallelism would have to earn it empirically.

LEVER 2 — `--timing` diagnostic
Add per-phase / per-step wall-clock instrumentation so a single state's ~4h is attributable (explore vs replay vs per-request waits vs gather). Diagnostic-only: must not alter captured content, determinism hashes, or verdicts.

LEVER 3 — per-request infra speedups + the O(N^2)->O(N) seed question
Shrinking a single state's ~4h is the harder problem and lives with the non-parallelism levers: per-request infra speedups, and the O(N^2)->O(N) seed question. NOTE the seed question is a FIDELITY TRADE (the adapter retired the seed accelerator so every surface is reached by pure navigation replay; re-introducing seeding to cut the O(N^2) replay cost trades fidelity for speed) — treat it as a design decision, not a free win.

BOTTOM LINE
- Safe and worth doing: parallelize across states on ISOLATED environments — ~3x faster, reliability-neutral/positive, rule-compliant.
- Not safe without proof (and likely counterproductive): ANY parallelism that shares a host's CPU/server/DB — it couples exactly the timing the determinism/arrival gates depend on, and Ticket c460 shows that coupling increases flakiness.
- Parallelism caps the safe win at ~3x (the x-states factor). Shrinking a single state's ~4h is the harder, separate problem (levers 2 + 3).

INVARIANTS (must hold for any item here)
HERMETIC: no item may make a scored run read anything beyond its declared run-inputs + the live prototype; do not change execution model mid-run.
PORTABLE: any new capability is keyed generically (roles / accessible names / manifest surface keys / generic flags), carries zero prototype literals, and lives in adapter/coverage-map/scenario data — engine stays prototype-agnostic.
REPEATABLE: any item must preserve byte-identical content-signature.json + k>=3 determinism + measurement positive controls vs the serial baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lever 1: a documented mechanism to run states on isolated environments concurrently, with a written argument (or a run) showing cross-state isolation does not share CPU/server/DB and therefore does not perturb any per-state content-signature.json or the k>=3 determinism / measurement positive controls.
- [ ] Adoption gate is enforced for ANY parallel config used in a scored run: the config is rejected unless it reproduces the serial baseline's content-signature.json and passes k>=3 determinism + measurement positive controls byte-identically (drift sentinel / determinism gate is the arbiter). Intra-host parallelism is NOT adopted unless it empirically clears this gate.
- [ ] Lever 2: `--timing` (or equivalent) emits per-phase/per-step wall-clock attribution for a single-state run, and is proven diagnostic-only (captured content, determinism hashes, and verdicts byte-identical with the flag on vs off).
- [ ] Lever 3: per-request infra speedups and the O(N^2)->O(N) seed question are written up as candidate optimizations, with the seed change explicitly flagged as a fidelity trade (decision recorded, not silently adopted).
- [ ] All three invariants (HERMETIC, PORTABLE, REPEATABLE) are preserved by whatever is implemented; no gate is weakened to gain speed.

title: OBSERVE deep-reach: step-level non-advance re-drive in reachByTrace (needs persisted per-step 'advanced') + gather.mjs arrival-retry
plan:
CONTEXT
Follow-on to ADR-0057 / c460-93f4-66cb-4aa9 (ashen-bully-acid). The run-0041 deep-reach arrival flake was
RESOLVED for the conformance gate by a depth-aware fresh-context arrival-retry bound (non-idempotent surfaces
get >=6 attempts; ADR-0057). That absorbs the stochastic dropped-step at the WHOLE-SURFACE level. Two deeper,
separable pieces were intentionally deferred and are tracked here.

SCOPE
1. reachByTrace step-level non-advance re-drive (the ROOT of the swallow). navigate.mjs reachByTrace has NO
   post-`act` assertion that the page advanced; with ~17 identical nth=0 "Save and continue" steps, a silent
   no-op lets the NEXT step's identical handle resolve on the un-advanced page, so the replay marches ahead and
   lands a non-terminal surface (clean non-arrival, no throw/drift/timeout). The clean fix re-drives a single
   non-advancing step in-context (cheaper than abandoning ~50 steps), GATED on whether the step was SUPPOSED to
   advance. BLOCKER: the persisted coverage-map trace records only {role,name,nth,kind,value} — NO per-step
   `advanced` flag (navigate.mjs reachStep at ~L282 omits it; verified absent in the snap coverage map). So this
   needs `advanced` (and/or `path`) persisted per step, which requires RE-RECORDING the coverage map (an
   LLM-driven exploration) and re-freezing the determinism baseline. Must stay PORTABLE (gate on the trace's own
   advanced flag, never a label/prototype literal) and preserve the loud-STOP guarantee (a permanently-stuck
   step still drifts).
2. gather.mjs arrival-retry. gather's deep-surface reach is single-pass (+ a best-effort drift re-probe) with NO
   fresh-context arrival-retry, so it is MORE fragile than conformance for the same surfaces (run-0041 MD
   post_submission_documents_en WARN that self-cleared on re-gather). Mirror the conformance
   depth-aware arrival-retry (arrivalRetryFor) into gather's reach.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage-map trace steps carry a per-step `advanced` (and/or `path`) signal, re-recorded without changing
      the frozen determinism baseline (or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented).
- [ ] reachByTrace re-drives a single non-advancing step bounded, gated on the trace's `advanced` flag (PORTABLE,
      no prototype literal); a step the trace recorded non-advancing is NOT re-driven; a permanently-stuck step
      still returns a LOUD drifted/non-arrival. Pinned by a new navigate-level unit test (fake page): silent-no-op
      heals via single-step re-drive; repeated-name swallow prevented; genuine non-advance STILL STOPs.
- [ ] gather.mjs deep-surface reach uses the same depth-aware fresh-context arrival-retry as conformance; a
      genuinely unreachable surface still WARNs/STOPs loudly. Pinned by a test.
- [ ] HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved; coupling-lint PASS.

title: Class A-4d: matcher robustness — legend-or-option-domain anchoring + verbose-option value-synonyms (mixed-status on real snap)
plan:
## What
A-4c grounded the matcher on REAL captured snap controls (group-aware extraction via groupCandidates) and closed AC2 for ESAP + destitute-migrant on real data. mixed-status does NOT yet resolve on real captured citizenship controls — a DOCUMENTED RED pinned in scenario-match-test.mjs (A-4c section 20). Two compounding causes (both verified by the A-4c plan-review):
1. LEGEND too generic: the citizenship group legend is 'What is <name>'s status?' — carries no immigration/citizenship/legal token, so question-type control resolution NO-CANDIDATEs at the legend layer.
2. VERBOSE OPTION labels: even forced past the legend, the real option names are sentences ('A non-citizen who can get food help For example, a green card.', 'Another kind of non-citizen You can still apply for food help.'); the value-synonym/polarity matcher ties (the token 'citizen' is contained in every option; polarity anti-tokens subtract equally) -> ambiguous.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] member-immigration-status resolves its determinative control on the REAL captured citizenship snapshot by anchoring on the legend OR on option-domain coverage (a group whose options collectively cover the question-type's value domain is that question-type's control).
- [ ] The 4 abstract immigration values bind to the 4 real verbose option labels distinctly (robust value-synonyms / a polarity rule that ignores a shared head token), with the existing collision/ambiguity guards intact.
- [ ] mixed-status resolves on the real captured citizenship controls; the A-4c 'documented RED' test is flipped to GREEN.
- [ ] No regression: the existing 46 scenario-match checks + the alien-mock portability stay green; no prototype literal leaks (lint ENGINE_FILES); deterministic.
- [ ] Closes A-4 AC2 for all three scenarios on real snap data.

## References
ADR-0039 (A-4c); scenario-match.mjs groupCandidates + pickBest polarity; the A-4c plan-review findings F1/F4b; run-0038 a4c-real-snapshots.json.

title: OBSERVE reach: goto-after-mutation commit-race on gated surfaces (wait-for-commit hardening)
plan:
## Context
The engine's map-replay reach reaches gated URLs by goto-ing them after a mutating step. On deep traces (e.g. benefit_estimate -> gated /apply/finances/preview, and signature_submission|es) under sustained load + dev-DB bloat, an earlier mutating step's (e.g. assets) server-side save has NOT committed when the trace goto-s the gated URL; the gated controller bounces to the incomplete section. Result: the surface lands on the wrong page (assets(en)/null(es)) and the masked-hash byte-identical gate flips. A4 classifies this automationArtifact (a back-pressured re-drive arrives), so it is NOT an engine correctness defect on the scored path, but it DOES break the byte-identical FULL-manifest >=k acceptance gate (A-5 AC-(a) / A-7 AC-1). Observed: signature_submission|es arrivals 2/3, hashStable=false; benefit_estimate 1/2 under load (4/4 in isolation).

## Expected
Before a goto to a gated URL that depends on a prior mutating step, the reach waits for that step's effect to be durably committed (server-confirmed), OR reaches the gated surface via the flow's natural forward nav rather than a raw goto. Deep gated reaches then arrive k/k byte-stable even on a loaded/bloated server.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A failing test reproduces the commit-race (goto gated surface immediately after a mutating step -> lands on the incomplete section) at the engine seam, hermetically (no live bloated server needed).
- [ ] Reach waits-for-commit (or natural-nav) before a gated goto; the reproduction test goes green.
- [ ] signature_submission|es + benefit_estimate reach k>=3 byte-identical under load (no capture-presence flip).
- [ ] Zero prototype literals introduced into the lint-guarded engine (portable); change is in the engine/adapter reach layer + tests only.
- [ ] ADR + PROVENANCE entry; dual adversarial subagent review (plan before code, code before commit).

## References
A-5 (b94c) + A-7 (0cc0) handoff comments; ADR-0047 (A4 classifyReachAnomaly); run-artifacts/run-0038/harness/acc-fullmanifest.{mjs,log} + probe-be-flake.mjs.

title: snap prototype destabilizes with accumulated use (no dev data-reset; unbounded growth -> slow/flaky)
plan:
## Context
The snap-oakhart-manual dev server has NO data-reset path and accumulates state without bound across audit/automation runs. Observed live (dev DB snap_oakhart_development @ :5434): pii_audit_events ~221k rows, benefit_applications ~2,349, household_members ~2,041, income_sources ~1,963, assets ~1,933, interviews ~1,828, authorized_representatives ~1,812 — all from repeated runs (~100+ apps/run). As the DB grows, the per-request work (per-request migration check + audit-event writes + association loads) slows progressively, which (i) inflates full-conformance wall-clock (~2.5h) and (ii) widens the goto-after-mutation commit-race window so deep gated reaches flake under load. Net: the app gets measurably less stable the more it is used, with no operator affordance to drain it.

## Expected
A dev/test affordance to reset accumulated state to a known clean baseline (rake task / endpoint guarded to non-prod), so repeated runs start from a stable, bounded DB. Per-run growth is bounded or reclaimable. Audit/automation suites can gate on a drained DB (make preflight-db already gates on a STABLE db; extend to a known-clean db).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A guarded, non-production data-reset (rake task or seeded reset) drains run-accumulated tables (benefit_applications + deps + pii_audit_events) to a clean baseline, preserving schema_migrations + the seed user.
- [ ] Documented in README dev/DB section + referenced by the audit harness preflight.
- [ ] After reset, a full-manifest reach run completes without the load/bloat-induced commit-race flake (ties to bug 8bda).
- [ ] Optionally: bound or document pii_audit_events growth in dev so the audit-write path does not dominate per-request latency.

## References
A-5 (b94c) + A-7 (0cc0) handoff comments (env-blocked full run); README native-postgres + make preflight-db; bug 8bda-cd4b-3459-46da (commit-race aggravated by this bloat).
